description: Preservation of religion — the orientation that gives every other good its meaning.
Preservation of life — without which no agent exists to bear the trust.
Preservation of intellect — the seat of moral discernment and responsibility.
Preservation of lineage — the continuity through which the function is carried forward.
Preservation of property — the material substrate on which the function is exercised.
Vital interests: bodily, biological, and material wellbeing.
Rational interests: knowledge, education, deliberation, intellectual flourishing.
Spiritual interests: orientation toward God, growth in virtue, the heart's openness to the divine.
